de.uka.ilkd.key.logic

Class WaryClashFreeSubst

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.ClashFreeSubst
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.WaryClashFreeSubst

  • public class WaryClashFreeSubst
extends ClashFreeSubst

    • Nested Class Summary

      • Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.ClashFreeSubst

        ClashFreeSubst.VariableCollectVisitor

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private boolean createQuantifier
      the formula should be prepended with a quantifier
      private int depth
      depth of recursion of the apply method
      private QuantifiableVariable newVar
      variable with which the original variable should be substituted below modalities
      private Term newVarTerm 
      private ImmutableSet<QuantifiableVariable> warysvars
      variables occurring within the original term and within the term to be substituted

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      WaryClashFreeSubst(QuantifiableVariable v, Term s, TermServices services) 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      private Term addWarySubst(Term t)
      Prepend the given term with a wary substitution (substituting newVar with getSubstitutedTerm())
      Term apply(Term t)
      substitute s for v in t, avoiding collisions by replacing bound variables in t if necessary.
      protected Term apply1(Term t)
      substitute s for v in t, avoiding collisions by replacing bound variables in t if necessary.
      private Term applyBelowModality(Term t)
      Apply the substitution to a term/formula below a modality or update
      private Term applyBelowTransformer(Term t)
      Apply the substitution to a term/formula below a transformer procedure
      private Term applyOnModality(Term t)
      Apply the substitution (that replaces a variable with a non-rigid term) on t, which has a modality as top-level operator.
      private Term applyOnTransformer(Term t)
      Apply the substitution (that replaces a variable with a term) on t, which has a transformer procedure as top-level operator.
      private Term applyOnUpdate(Term t)
      Apply the substitution (that replaces a variable with a non-rigid term) on t, which has an update operator as top-level operator.
      private void createVariable()
      Create a new logic variable to be used for substitutions below modalities
      private void findUsedVariables(Term t)
      Determine a set of variables that do already appear within t or the substituted term, and whose names should not be used for free variables
      private Term substWithNewVar(Term t)
      Rename the original variable to be substituted to newVar

        applyOnSubterm, getSingleArray, getSubstitutedTerm, getVariable, newVarFor, subTermChanges

    • Field Detail

      • depth

        private int depth
        depth of recursion of the apply method

      • createQuantifier

        private boolean createQuantifier
        the formula should be prepended with a quantifier

      • newVar

        private QuantifiableVariable newVar
        variable with which the original variable should be substituted below modalities

      • newVarTerm

        private Term newVarTerm

      • warysvars

        private ImmutableSet<QuantifiableVariable> warysvars
        variables occurring within the original term and within the term to be substituted

    • Constructor Detail

      • WaryClashFreeSubst

        public WaryClashFreeSubst(QuantifiableVariable v,
                          Term s,
                          TermServices services)

    • Method Detail

      • apply

        public Term apply(Term t)
        substitute s for v in t, avoiding collisions by replacing bound variables in t if necessary.
        Overrides:
        apply in class ClashFreeSubst

      • findUsedVariables

        private void findUsedVariables(Term t)
        Determine a set of variables that do already appear within t or the substituted term, and whose names should not be used for free variables

      • createVariable

        private void createVariable()
        Create a new logic variable to be used for substitutions below modalities

      • apply1

        protected Term apply1(Term t)
        substitute s for v in t, avoiding collisions by replacing bound variables in t if necessary. It is assumed, that t contains a free occurrence of v.
        Overrides:
        apply1 in class ClashFreeSubst

      • applyOnModality

        private Term applyOnModality(Term t)
        Apply the substitution (that replaces a variable with a non-rigid term) on t, which has a modality as top-level operator. This is done by creating a (top-level) existential quantifier. This method is only called from apply1 for substitutions with non-rigid terms PRECONDITION: warysvars != null

      • applyOnTransformer

        private Term applyOnTransformer(Term t)
        Apply the substitution (that replaces a variable with a term) on t, which has a transformer procedure as top-level operator. This is done by creating a (top-level) existential quantifier. This method is only called from apply1 for substitutions with non-rigid terms PRECONDITION: warysvars != null

      • applyOnUpdate

        private Term applyOnUpdate(Term t)
        Apply the substitution (that replaces a variable with a non-rigid term) on t, which has an update operator as top-level operator. This is done by creating a (top-level) existential quantifier. This method is only called from apply1 for substitutions with non-rigid terms PRECONDITION: warysvars != null

      • applyBelowModality

        private Term applyBelowModality(Term t)
        Apply the substitution to a term/formula below a modality or update

      • applyBelowTransformer

        private Term applyBelowTransformer(Term t)
        Apply the substitution to a term/formula below a transformer procedure

      • addWarySubst

        private Term addWarySubst(Term t)
        Prepend the given term with a wary substitution (substituting newVar with getSubstitutedTerm())

      • substWithNewVar

        private Term substWithNewVar(Term t)
        Rename the original variable to be substituted to newVar
